title: Because, So, But, Although
description: The connectors that turn boring sentences into paragraphs. BECAUSE (reason), SO (result), BUT (contrast), ALTHOUGH (concession).
summary_pt_br: Conectivos básicos: BECAUSE (porque — razão), SO (então — resultado), BUT (mas — contraste), ALTHOUGH (embora — concessão). Regra: BECAUSE + oração, BECAUSE OF + substantivo.

scenes.text_en:
'I stayed home BECAUSE it was raining.' (reason)
'It was raining, SO I stayed home.' (result)
'I wanted to go, BUT it was raining.' (contrast)
'ALTHOUGH it was raining, I went out.' (concession)
Four words. Four meanings. Careful with placement.
scenes.text_pt:
'Fiquei em casa PORQUE estava chovendo.' (razão)
'Estava chovendo, ENTÃO fiquei em casa.' (resultado)
'Eu queria sair, MAS estava chovendo.' (contraste)
'EMBORA estivesse chovendo, saí.' (concessão)
Quatro palavras. Quatro sentidos.

scenes.title: Because vs Because of
scenes.body_en: BECAUSE + full clause (subject + verb). 'Because I was tired.' BECAUSE OF + noun. 'Because of the rain.' Common mistake: 'Because of I was tired' — WRONG. Use 'because' with a clause or 'because of' with a noun, never mixed.
scenes.body_pt: BECAUSE + oração (sujeito + verbo). BECAUSE OF + substantivo. 'Because I was tired' / 'Because of the rain'. Nunca misture.

examples.en: Because of the rain, I stayed.
Because it was raining, I stayed.

exercise.question: 'I was tired, ___ I went to bed.' (result)
Complete: 'I stayed home ___ ___ the rain.' (because of + noun)

exercise.explanation: Five connectors. Each has a distinct role.

exercise.question: Complete: "I am ___, I did not have much time."
exercise.explanation: The full phrase is: "I am sorry, I did not have much time."

exercise.question: Pick the best response. (HOWEVER.)
options: I like your idea, however I find it hard.
exercise.explanation: The correct response is: "I like your idea, however I find it hard."

scenes.title: Essay feedback
scenes.pt_summary: Conectivos — and, but, because, so, however.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Your essay is good, but too short.
turns.pt: Seu texto é bom, mas curto.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I am sorry, I did not have much time.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Use more linking words, such as 'however'.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I like your idea, however I find it hard.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I study English because I love it.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I practice every day, so I am improving.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Although I am tired, I still study.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Excellent. Your writing is getting stronger.

scenes.title: Listening story — Because, So, But, Although
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Zara and a student. The connectors that turn boring sentences into paragraphs. BECAUSE (reason), SO (result), BUT (contrast), ALTHOUGH (concession). Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Your essay is good, but too short.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: I am sorry, I did not have much time.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Use more linking words, such as 'however'.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: I like your idea, however I find it hard.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: I study English because I love it.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Zara. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Because, So, But, Although". The connectors that turn boring sentences into paragraphs. BECAUSE (reason), SO (result), BUT (contrast), ALTHOUGH (concession). Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Because, So, But, Although". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Because, So, But, Although'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Because, So, But, Although".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: Perfect English Grammar
sources.pt_hint: Regras de gramática + exercícios interativos gratuitos.

sources.label: British Council · A2
sources.pt_hint: Leituras A2 com exercícios de compreensão.
